topic: My thermostat resets itself to sixty eight every night at exactly three fourteen AM
title: My Thermostat Resets Itself to 68 Every Night at Exactly 3:14 AM | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: My thermostat resets itself to sixty eight degrees every night at exactly three fourteen AM.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of an old brass round wall thermostat with a mercury dial pointed at sixty eight degrees, dim wall paint behind, nighttime ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Old house. Cape Cod. Bought it last fall. No smart home anything.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior of a single story Cape Cod house at dusk in late autumn, bare trees in the yard, one window lit, no cars in the driveway, vertical 9:16 framing
close detail of a vintage round mechanical thermostat on a hallway wall, brass dial centered, dim hallway lamp light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: First noticed it in November. Cold woke me up. Dial sitting at sixty eight.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a bedside alarm clock showing three fifteen AM in red digital numerals, a corner of a quilted blanket visible at the edge of frame, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Set it back. Went to sleep.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a hand reaching toward the brass dial of a wall thermostat in dim light, only the hand visible, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Woke at three fifteen AM. Cold again. Dial at sixty eight again.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of the same brass thermostat dial showing sixty eight degrees, viewed from a low angle in dim early morning light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Stayed up the next night. Watched it.
scenes.image_prompt: wide interior of a darkened hallway at night, a small distant glow from a bedside lamp at the far end, no figures visible, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Three fourteen. The dial turned itself. No one was near it.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of the brass mechanical thermostat dial visibly mid rotation, no hand on it, dim ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Hired an electrician. He said no mechanism in there can do that.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot of an electrician's hands holding a multimeter against the back of a removed thermostat plate, exposed wiring visible, dim workshop light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Checked county records. The previous owner died here. Hypothermia. February.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of a yellowed paper document on a kitchen table, an old fountain pen lying across it, only the corner visible, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Police report said the heat had been set to sixty eight.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of a typed police report page partially obscured, low desk lamp light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I keep mine at seventy two. Every morning at three fifteen it is sixty eight.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of the brass thermostat at three fifteen AM showing sixty eight degrees, a blurred bedroom doorway visible in the background, no figures, vertical 9:16 framing
